title: Looking for backtesting websites ( preferably cheap or free ) , which also let me journal my backtested trades on automation
author: Ok_Seesaw9275
selftext: Hey everyone , I have decided to re enter the market after a major setback 3 months back , this time I have decided to commence with backtesting my strategies first , refine the win rate , trade demo and only then go to the live market. For this I need suggestions for free backtesting websites that also let me journal my backtested trades on automation without me having to journal them manually . With that automated journalling , I get to find common mistakes and patterns in my losing trades , thats the main reason i dont wanna journal manually as we often seems to overlook any common pointers . Happy Trading , Cheers!

comments.author: PuzzleheadedDoor6336
comments.body: Like yeah you have set the right path to first backtest your strategy only then trade demo and then live , you may use free alternatives like Tradexis, Tradezella or simply Trading view , once you are done backtesting , the first two also let you journal your trades , i reckon they have the option to let your automate the journalling thing so yeah you may try em out.

replies.author: Ok_Seesaw9275
replies.body: sure man , appreciate it, will see which one 's UI suits me the best and may try them accordingly.

comments.author: vammmmmooooosssss
comments.body: Alr , not so sure if they let you journal your backtested trades , but they r decent for backtesting - Tradersync ,Tradexis and Trading edge, for journalling stonk journal is a fine choice aswl but yeah i dont think they let you automate the journalling thing

comments.author: Scary_Claim_8324
comments.body: Why do want to automate the Journaling thing in the 1st place?

comments.author: BudgetTraditional402
comments.body: the way u are approaching the market, this time is appreciable, immediately live trading is why most people keep failing themselves, u can use trading view ig

comments.author: Old_Combination_8691
comments.body:
Automated journalling - Tradezella , Trading edge , Tradexis . Free backtesting - gocharting , trading view , tradezella , tradexis and backtesting max let u backtest so  , happy trading to you too , cheers !

Ps - Automated journalling is great however if you are just starting out , excel sheets is sonething u need to be using , like i would recommend that as you get to know what you are actually doing in the market

comments.author: Horizon_trade_ai
comments.body:
I've tested quite a few of them and honestly the biggest problem is fragmentation.

TradingView is great for charts and basic backtesting, TradeZella is solid for journaling, and there are a bunch of AI tools for research, but I still haven't found a platform that connects the whole workflow together.

The idea of combining backtesting, journaling, trade analysis, and AI-generated insights in one place makes a lot of sense. If Horizon Trade is aiming for that, I'd be interested in seeing how it develops.

For me the biggest missing feature is automatic pattern recognition across both live and backtested trades. I want software that can tell me why my setups work, not just track them.

comments.author: Mother_Steak_8709
comments.body: Why would you want to automate journalling your trades in the 1st place?

replies.author: Ok_Seesaw9275
replies.body: okay so the thing is, I had been journalling my trades on excel sheets, but it 's honestly hassle having to write about each trade manually and also the mistake recognition thing is the main reason I wanna switch to  automated journalling , it would save me a great ton of time in manually jotting down a trade 's brief and also help detect any common mistake patterns as well
